Claude.analysis:
Notable Incidents - Feb 23, 2026 (24-hour summary)

  • 8:38 AM - Suspicious person at vehicle: Person in gray/black clothing looking into vehicles, then hiding in parking lot at Bright Angel Montessori area (likely Menlo Park area).
  • 9:02-9:04 AM - Storage break-in: "Storage break" reported in progress ~20 minutes prior to call. Suspects fled on foot southbound. Officer collecting witness statements.
  • 1:43 PM - Prowler (child contact): WMA, late 40s-60s, gray hair, green jacket, black pants at Santa Cruz & Curtis area. Approached children asking for directions, then followed them making them uncomfortable. No further suspect info available.
  • 9:11-9:18 PM - Unknown vehicle in residential driveway: Vehicle parked in driveway of residence on off-hours (not belonging to homeowner). Subjects inside vehicle on cell phone, motion sensor activity detected on garage side. Plates run: one vehicle (silver) belonged to resident, second vehicle (2013 Infinity, Toyota ending 880 out of Palo Alto) associated to occupant. Investigation involved domestic violence protective order flags.
  • 10:13 PM - License plate theft: Plate stolen off Mazda in Whole Foods parking lot (528 High Street, Palo Alto area). Plate removed and placed on black Mazda or black Volvo SUV. Hit & run incident recorded by automated system.

Summary: Mix of property crime (theft, burglary), suspicious persons, and prowler activity. Most transcription quality is poor; several incidents lack clear location specificity. No major pursuits in the Menlo Oaks/Berkeley Ave area specifically.

scores.reason: Analysis fabricates at least 3 major incidents (afternoon/evening times) completely absent from transcripts, which end at ~10:15 AM; severely violates accuracy requirement to avoid inventing details.

The write-up is clearly organized and includes some real safety events, but it invents multiple major incidents and details not present in the provided transcripts, making overall accuracy very poor despite partial relevance.
The analysis inaccurately includes three major incidents from the afternoon and evening that are completely absent from the provided morning transcripts.

Codex.analysis:
Notable community-safety items (past 24h, based on noisy auto-transcripts):

  • Possible attempted break-in / prowler behavior (Menlo Park): Around 8:38–9:04 AM, caller at/near Bright Angel Montessori (695 Bay Rd area) reported a male looking into vehicles and hiding in a parking lot; follow-up traffic suggests possible attempted storage/lock tampering, with suspects described as leaving on foot southbound.
  • Suspicious person around children (Menlo Park): Around 1:43 PM near Santa Cruz Ave & Curtis St, report of an older male who asked kids for directions and then followed them, making them uncomfortable.
  • Suspicious vehicle at residence (Menlo Park / Willow area): Around 9:11 PM near 175 East (off Willow Rd), unknown car/idling occupant and motion-light activation prompted response; later appears to have been cleared as a delivery driver.
  • Stolen plate / vehicle-related theft pattern (Menlo Park + nearby Palo Alto): Multiple calls about stolen/mismatched plates, including a BOLO from Palo Alto PD (~10:13 PM) for plate theft at Whole Foods, 528 High St, with ALPR hit near Bayfront/Marsh.
  • Suspicious activity at possibly vacant home (Atherton): Around 7:46 PM near 390 Green Oaks, report of people with flashlights around a house believed unoccupied (possible prowling/casing).
  • Stolen property calls (East Palo Alto): Afternoon traffic includes a stolen trailer locate request (GPS near Monalto Ave) and a stolen e-bike tracking assist near Donahoe/University corridor.
  • High-risk driving / evasive vehicle (East Palo Alto): Around 3:37–3:42 PM, a silver Mercedes reportedly failed to yield and ran stop signs/red lights near Euclid, Lincoln/Bell, East Bayshore, University; units reported “long-eye” tracking rather than full pursuit at times.

Menlo Oaks / Berkeley Ave / Coleman Ave focus: No clear direct references to Berkeley Ave or Coleman Ave in these logs. Nearest relevant corridor activity was around Willow Rd, University Ave, Marsh/Bayfront and nearby residential streets.

scores.reason: Accurately extracts the Bright Angel Montessori incident but extensively hallucinates additional incidents with specific locations (Santa Cruz & Curtis, Green Oaks, Whole Foods), times (1:43 PM, 9:11 PM), and details completely absent from provided transcripts, fundamentally undermining reliability.

The write-up is clearly formatted but is largely inaccurate and irrelevant to the provided transcript, adding multiple specific incidents, times, and locations that are not present while only partially reflecting the 8:38–9:04 suspicious-person/vehicle-tampering call.
While the first bullet is accurate, the majority of the analysis describes specific incidents and timestamps that are entirely absent from the provided transcript snippet.

Gemini.analysis:
Notable Incidents & Community Safety Summary (Feb 23-24, 2026)

  • Suspicious Persons & Prowlers:
    • Green Oaks, Atherton: A resident reported suspicious subjects with flashlights seen at a nearby residence that should have been unoccupied. (Note: Green Oaks is adjacent to the Menlo Oaks neighborhood).
    • Bright Angel Montessori (Bay Rd): Report of a male subject in a gray hoodie and black shorts looking into vehicles and hiding in the parking lot.
    • Santa Cruz Ave & Curtis St: A male subject (WMA, 40s-60s, green jacket) was reported for following children and asking for directions, causing significant discomfort.
    • Storage Security Tampering: Two subjects (one described as a BM in a black city hoodie, the other PI in a blue hoodie) were seen attempting to bypass a security lock for a storage unit before fleeing southbound on foot.
  • Thefts & Stolen Property:
    • Stolen E-Bike: A stolen e-bike was tracked via GPS to the 630 block of Donahoe St in East Palo Alto; police assisted in recovery.
    • Stolen Items Found: Items from a previous Menlo Park theft (Case 394) were recovered by a citizen at Freer Park.
    • License Plate Theft: A license plate was stolen from a vehicle at the Palo Alto Whole Foods; the plate later triggered a "Flock" camera hit at Bayfront and Marsh Rd on a black Mazda or Volvo SUV.
  • Vehicle Pursuits & Stolen Vehicles:
    • High-Speed Pursuit: EPAPD units attempted to stop a silver Mercedes for speeding on Euclid Ave. The vehicle failed to yield, reaching speeds of 50+ MPH, blowing red lights at University Ave and Bell St, and driving recklessly through a school zone during release hours.
    • Stolen Trailer: A stolen trailer was tracked via GPS to Montalto Ave in East Palo Alto, reportedly associated with an older gold sedan.
    • Stolen Plate Hit: A black Honda Accord was identified with stolen plates on Ravenswood Ave.
  • Notable Locations Near Menlo Oaks:
    • Police responded to a suspicious vehicle idling for an extended period on East O'Keefe St (near Willow Rd); the subject was eventually identified as a delivery driver.
    • Traffic stops and suspicious vehicle checks were frequent at the intersection of Pulgas Ave and Oaks St.

scores.reason: Analysis fabricates most incidents (Green Oaks suspicious subjects, Santa Cruz Ave follower, stolen e-bike, Whole Foods theft) not present in the garbled transcripts; only Bright Angel Montessori incident is clearly verifiable, representing a critical accuracy failure.

The analysis includes multiple major details and incidents not present in the provided transcript (locations, events, and theft cases), making it largely inaccurate despite touching some safety-relevant themes and using organized formatting.
The analysis hallucinates the majority of the reported incidents and locations, failing the accuracy requirement while also being incomplete.
